info.title: Metadata Management (Cloud Edition)
info.description: The Metadata Management API provides access to information about the location, attributes, quality, and sensitivity of data. With this information, you can make informed decisions about which datasets to publish and determine who has access to use or view information about the datasets.

x-sap-shortText: Manage your metadata across different systems.

externalDocs.description: Data Governance User Guide for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud

x-servers.description: Metadata Management endpoint in an SAP Data Intelligence Cloud instance.

Cloud Instance.description: The name of the SAP Data Intelligence Cloud instance.

Landscape.description: The landscape where the SAP Data Intelligence Cloud instance is running.

get.summary: Get containers and datasets under a container identified by containerId
get.description: Get containers and datasets under a container identified by containerId. For the root container, use the keyword connectionRoot without quotes for containerId

parameters.name: containerId
parameters.description: The container identifier

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: * container -  Indicates that only child containers are requested. * dataset - Indicates that only child datasets are requested. * undefined - Both containers and datasets are returned.

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description: The expression to filter the returned entries. The following syntax is supported: the filter expression can be any conjunction of a single predicate per property. A 400 error is returned when a filter is incorrect or multiple predicates for a single property are provided. A filter can be applied on the following properties: (1) name : the name of subcontainers or datasets under this folder, (2) connectionType: the type of the remote connection from which the dataset is published. Simple predicates can be : (1) equality predicate: (for example, connectionType eq 'HANA') (2) contains predicate (for example, contains(name,'CUST') or, (3) IN predicate (for example, connectionType in ['HANA', 'MYSQL'])

count.description: The number of objects (datasets and containers)

400.description: Bad request e.g. caused by invalid filter argument

get.description: Get all connections which can be accessed via Metadata Management. Additional filters and query parameters can be applied to include only relevant connections, see below.

parameters.name: connectionTypes
parameters.description: Connection types to filter on, for example, "BW,HANA_XS". When several connection types are passed, connections belonging to all the types are returned.

parameters.name: serviceProvider
parameters.description: Service provider to filter on, for example, `com.sap.dh.flowagent`.

parameters.name: serviceType
parameters.description: Service type to filter on, for example, `statusCheck`.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Connection tags to filter on, for example, "http,application". In case several tags are passed, connections having any of the passed tags are returned

parameters.name: licenseRelevant
parameters.description: If true, return the connection that is taken into account for the license measurement only

parameters.name: showMetadataImport
parameters.description: If true, return also connections of type 'METADATA_IMPORT' because they are hidden by default.

post.description: Add a new connection that can be accessed through Metadata Management. The required connection type can be retrieved via /api/v1/catalog/connectionTypes. See below.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Connection Definition

200.description: Connection has been created, but not all requested options could be applied. Body contains options which could be applied.

409.description: Connection already exists under given name

get.description: Get a connection by its ID and retrieve detailed information regarding the connection.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection ID

put.description: Update a connection be referencing it via its ID and change the fields specified in publicConnectionUpdate.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection ID

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Connection Definition

200.description: Connection has been updated, but not all requested options could be applied. Body contains options which could be applied.

delete.description: Permanently removes all information about this connection.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection ID

get.description: Connection type specifies the type of system a connection points to.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Connection type tags to filter on, for example, "http,application". When several tags are passed, connection types having any of the passed tags are returned

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: If true, return enabled connection types only. If false, return non-enabled connection types only. If undefined, do not filter on the "enabled" flag.

post.description: Import datasets into the catalog via API. The schema of the imported data is defined via ImportedDataset which is a subset of the definitions AdaptedDataset/RemoteDataset used by the catalog to return data. This is an asynchronous operation. It only works for datasets where the connection type is METADATA_IMPORT.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Datasets to be imported

400.description: Bad request, for example, publicAdaptedDataset syntax does not match the schema

get.summary: Get the status on an ImportTask
get.description: Get the status on an asynchronous ImportTask SUCCESS, PENDING, RUNNING, or ERROR. If it is ERROR, it provides additional information.

parameters.name: importTaskId
parameters.description: ID of the importTask that was received as response from a POST call against /api/v1/catalog/datasets

get.description: Export data from the catalog by specifying the dataset which should be exported

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the dataset to be exported. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

delete.description: Delete a dataset from the catalog. Currently restricted to datasets which were added to the catalog via API.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the dataset to be deleted. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

403.description: A delete operation on the specified dataset is a prohibited action.

404.description: No dataset under the provided connectionId and qualifiedName.

get.description: Export data from the catalog by specifying the container which should be exported. Specifying the root ('/') of a connection exports the entire metadata content for this connection.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the container to be exported. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

count.description: Total number of datasets for the given qualified name

delete.description: Delete a container from the catalog. All containers and datasets included will be deleted as well. Currently restricted to datasets which were added to the catalog via API.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the container to be deleted. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

403.description: A delete operation on the specified container is a prohibited action.

404.description: No container under the provided connectionId and qualifiedName.

get.summary: Get a fact sheet using a qualified path on a connection
get.description: A fact sheet returns detailed information about a dataset such as its schema or annotations. It returns the results from the last succesful profiling run, when available.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID of a dataset

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of a dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

get.description: Export lineage information from the catalog. Supports filtering on connection ID and qualified name. The export includes all transformations where datasets specified in the filter expression participate either as source or as target. If no filters are specified, the entire lineage in the catalog is exported. Count indicates the amount of computation nodes that match a given query.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedNameFilter
parameters.description: Case-sensitive filter expression on the qualified name of the datasets to be exported. Filters on the names of datasets only return those names matching the query. Use wildcard semantics with '%' as the wildcard character to match zero or more characters. Wildcard can be escaped with a leading '@', for example 'data@%' matches the string data%. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name filter parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

publicComputationNodes.description: List of computation nodes describing lineage information.

count.description: Amount of computation nodes that match the given connection ID or qualified name

get.description: Retrieve all rules grouped by category. It is possible to filter the rules by rulebook ID.

parameters.name: rulebookId
parameters.description: Optional expression to only return the rules for a given rulebook identified by its ID.

get.description: Retrieve a single rule and all its details identified by the rule ID.

parameters.name: ruleId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of a rule

get.summary: Retrieve a list of rulebook information
get.description: Retrieve a list of rulebook information including a summary info for each rulebook.
Retrieve a rulebook and all details of this rulebook according to the PublicRulebookDetails schema.

parameters.name: rulebookId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of a rulebook

get.summary: Retrieve rulebook results grouped by datasets
get.description: Retrieve rulebook results grouped by datasets including detailed execution results for each dataset.

parameters.name: rulebookId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of a rulebook

get.description: Get a list of all datasets which are bound to at least one rule in a rulebook.

parameters.name: rulebookId
parameters.description: Optional filter expression to filter the datasets based on the rulebook ID.

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: Optional filter expression to filter the datasets based on the qualified name of the dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Optional filter expression to filter the datasets based on the connection id of the dataset.

get.summary: Get the list of scheduled tasks
get.description: Get the list of scheduled tasks. The list can be filtered based on thr task type.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter results based on the scheduled task type, for example, Rulebook

get.description: List all defined tag hierarchies. Use a search pattern to filter the hierarchies to return.

parameters.name: $search
parameters.description: An optional search string using * as a wildcard to filter hierarchies by their name and/or by the name of their tags. A hierarchy is returned when one of the following conditions holds: (1) the name matches the search string, or (2) it contains a tag whose name matches the search string. Hierarchies with matching names (case 1) are returned at the top of the list.

tagHierarchies.description: List of tag hierarchies. If a $search parameter is used to filter the list, then each matching hierarchy is returned with matching information in the name of the hierarchy or an internal tag.

defaultHierarchyId.description: Default hierarchy set by the user

post.description: Create a new tag hierarchy using a name and a description.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The name and description of the new hierarchy.

400.description: Bad request. For example, invalid / duplicated hierarchy name.

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to serialization error.

get.summary: Get the description and content of a tag hierarchy
get.description: Get the description and content of a tag hierarchy. Content is given only when requested with parameter withTags=true.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the hierarchy.

parameters.name: withTags
parameters.description: An optional parameter. If withTags = true then all tags defined in the hierarchy are returned. Otherwise, only the properties of the hierarchy are provided.

put.description: Update an existing tag hierarchy. The hierarchy to be updated is identified using its ID.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the hierarchy.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The name and description of the hierarchy to update.

400.description: Bad request. For example, invalid / duplicated hierarchy name.

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to a serialization error.

delete.description: Delete a hierarchy. If force = true then the hierarchy is deleted even if the tags are used for tagging. In this case, all tags are removed.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the hierarchy.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: If force = true then the hierarchy is deleted even if the tags are used for tagging. All corresponding tags are deleted. If force = false or is undefined then we first check that there aren't any objects with one or more tags from this hierarchy. If there are some tagged objects, an error is raised.

400.description: Bad request. Mode is not equal to force and some objects have tags from this hierarchy.

post.summary: Add a new tag in the hierarchy
post.description: Add a new tag in the hierarchy. The hierarchy is identified by its ID.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the hierarchy.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The description of the new tag in the hierarchy

400.description: Bad request. For example, invalid / duplicated tag name.

404.description: No hierarchy with this ID or tag parentId exists

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to a serialization error. This may occur when another user is updating the same hierarchy.

put.summary: Update details of a tag in the hierarchy
put.description: Update name, description, and color of a tag in the hierarchy.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the hierarchy.

parameters.name: tagId
parameters.description: The ID of the tag.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The new name or description of the tag.

400.description: Bad request. For example, invalid / duplicated tag name.

404.description: No hierarchy or tag with this ID.

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to serialization error. This can occur when another user is updating the same hierarchy.

delete.summary: Remove a tag from its hierarchy
delete.description: Remove a tag from its hierarchy. If the tag is an intermediate node in the tag hierarchy, then the whole subtree is deleted. If force = true, the tag and its subtree are deleted even if those tags are not used for tagging. In force = false or is undefined, an error is raised if some tags are in use.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the hierarchy.

parameters.name: tagId
parameters.description: the The ID the tag.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: If force = true, the tag and its descendants are deleted even if they are used for tagging. The deleted tag and its descendants are removed from the tagged objects. If force parameter is not provided or is equal to false, then we check that here aren't any objects tagged with this tag or any of its descendants. If there aren't any tagged objects, the whole subtree is deleted. Otherwise, an error is raised.

400.description: Bad request. Mode is not force and some tags are existing.

404.description: No hierarchies or tags with this ID.

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to a serialization error. This can occur when another user is updating the same hierarchy.

get.summary: Give details on all tags attached to the input dataset
get.description: Get all tags associated to the given dataset or to one of its attributes. Tags on the dataset are grouped by their corresponding hierarchy. Tags on attributes are grouped by attribute. If an attribute is tagged using several hierarchies, then the tags attached to this attribute are grouped by their corresponding hierarchy.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: tagHierarchies
parameters.description: A list of hierarchy IDs separated by ','. It can be used to filter the hierarchies.

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to a serialization error. This may occur when another user is using the same tagging end point on the same object and with the same hierarchy.

post.summary: Add a tag on the dataset
post.description: Add a tag on the dataset. The dataset is identified using a connection ID and a qualified name.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The tag hierarchy ID.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The tag ID.

201.description: Success. Returns the list of all tags on the dataset

404.description: The dataset or hierarchy was not found

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. Retrying the same request is likely to succeed.

put.summary: Change the tagging on the dataset
put.description: Change the tagging of the dataset by replacing all tags from a hierarchy with new tags from the same hierarchy. Only tags directly attached to the input dataset are considered; tags on attributes are not considered. Remove previous tags of the input hierarchy that are attached to the dataset. Tags given in the body are then attached to the input dataset. If a tag does not belong to the input hierarchy, it is not considered for tagging.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the tag hierarchy for which tags should be replaced

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The new list of tags from the input hierarchy used to tag the object.

200.description: Success; returns the list of new tags for the dataset

404.description: The dataset or hierarchy not found

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting  concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to a serialization error. This can occur when another user is using the same tagging end point on the same object and with the same hierarchy.

delete.summary: Remove a tag from a dataset
delete.description: Remove a tag from a dataset using hierarchy id and tag id.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: the tag hierarchy id

parameters.name: tagId
parameters.description: the tag id.

200.description: Success. Returns the list of all tags on the dataset

404.description: dataset not found or hierarchy not found

409.description: operation aborted due to conflict concurrent operation. retrying the same request is likely to succeed.

post.summary: Add a tag on the attribute
post.description: Add a tag on the attribute given by a qualified name.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the remote dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: attributeQualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the attribute that uniquely identifies the attribute in the dataset schema. No encoding needed.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: the tag hierarchy id.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The description of the new tag to add in the hierarchy

201.description: Success; Returns the list of all tags on the attribute.

404.description: Attribute or hierarchy was not found

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. Retrying the same request is likely to succeed.

put.summary: Change the tagging on the dataset attribute
put.description: Change the tagging on the dataset attribute by replacing all tags from a hierarchy by adding new tags from the same hierarchy. Only tags directly attached to the input attribute are considered. Remove previous tags of the input hierarchy that are attached to the attribute. Tags given in the body are then attached to the input attribute. If a tag does not belong to the input hierarchy, it is not considered for tagging.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the remote dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: attributeQualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the attribute that uniquely identifies the attribute in the dataset schema. No encoding needed.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: The ID of the tag hierarchy for which tags should be replaced.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The new list of tags of the input hierarchy that should be used to tag the object.

200.description: Success; returns the list of new tags for the attribute.

404.description: The dataset or hierarchy was not found

409.description: Operation ended due to a conflicting concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is ended due to a serialization error. This can occur when another user is using the same tagging end point on the same object and with the same hierarchy.

delete.summary: Remove a tag from an attribute in a dataset using hierarchy id and tag id
delete.description: Remove a tag from the give attribute of given hierarchy id and tag id.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: The connection ID

parameters.name: qualifiedName
parameters.description: The qualified name of the remote dataset. The qualified name parameter includes the path and the name of a dataset. An encoding is used where all characters must be UTF-8 encoded except for A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ! ~ * ' ( ) In the qualified name parameter, all values have to be double encoded and slashes that separate values should be single encoded. Such an encoding is necessary as otherwise special characters such as a slash could be interpreted as part of the URL string. Functions which can create such an encoding are the encodeURIComponent() function in JavaScript or urllib.parse.quote() in Python. Example /path/myfile#.csv -> encodeURIComponent(`/${encodeURIComponent("path")}/${encodeURIComponent("myfile#.csv")}`) -> %2Fpath%2Fmyfile%2523.csv

parameters.name: attributeQualifiedName
parameters.description: the qualified name of the attribute that uniquely identifies the attribute in the dataset schema. No encoding needed.

parameters.name: hierarchyId
parameters.description: the id of the tag hierarchy for which tags should be removed

parameters.name: tagId
parameters.description: the id of the the tag.

200.description: Success. Returns the list of all tags for the dataset

404.description: dataset not found or hierarchy not found

409.description: operation aborted due to conflict concurrent operation. A 409 error is raised when the underlying transaction is aborted due to serialization error. This may occur when another user, is using the same tagging end point on the same object and with the same hierarchy

publicNodeEntry.description: Common attributes shared by every object that can be returned by a browse or search call

name.description: The name of the object as seen by the user

qualifiedName.description: The full qualified name of object in the remote source (unique within the connection)

parentQualifiedName.description: The path of the parent container in the catalog. For published objects under root it is "root". For remote objects under root it is /

description.description: A description of the object (auto-proposed from the remote system)

publicPublishedNodeEntry.description: Represents a node returned by browsing or searching the catalog

id.description: A UUID technically identifying the published object

parentId.description: A UUID identifying the parent container. NULL in case of a container under root

connectionType.description: The type of the connection from which the object is extracted (optional)

connectionId.description: The ID of the connection from which the object is extracted

remoteContainerPath.description: The remote path to reach the corresponding object.

catalogObjectType.description: Use this option when the dataset is an adapted dataset or simply refers to a remote dataset

publicPublishedContainerEntry.description: Represents a container returned while browsing published content

publicPublishedDatasetEntry.description: Represents a Dataset<->container connection returned while browsing the catalog

publicConnection.description: Contains all information describing a connection.

type.description: The type of the connection, for example, "BW,HANA_XS".

gatewayId.description: The ID of a gateway used for establishing the connection, for example, SAP Cloud Connector.

cloudConnectorLocationId.description: The location ID of SAP Cloud Connector, for example, Walldorf.

licenseRelevant.description: Indicates whether a connection is relevant for software licensing purposes.

contentData.description: Contains connection information (for example hostname, port).

tags.description: Describes additional properties or supported services of a connection.

tagsFromType.description: Contains tags derived from the connection type.

dependentConnections.description: List of connections which are dependent on this connection.

publicConnectionStatus.description: Indicates the current status of the connection, for example, if it can be reached.

publicConnectionCreate.description: Containing all information that can be provided when creating a connection.

gatewayId.description: The ID of a gateway used for establishing the connection, for example, SAP Cloud Connector.

cloudConnectorLocationId.description: The location ID of SAP Cloud Connector, for example, Walldorf.

licenseRelevant.description: Indicates if connection is relevant for software licensing purposes.

contentData.description: Contains connection information, for example, hostname, port.

tags.description: Describes additional properties or supported services of a connection.

publicConnectionUpdate.description: Contains the information that can be provided when updating a connection.

type.description: The type of the connection, for example, "BW,HANA_XS".

gatewayId.description: The ID of a gateway used for establishing the connection, for example, SAP Cloud Connector.

cloudConnectorLocationId.description: The location ID of SAP Cloud Connector, for example, Walldorf.

licenseRelevant.description: Indicates whether a connection is relevant for software licensing purposes.

tags.description: Describes additional properties or supported services of a connection.

contentData.description: Contains connection information, for example, hostname, port.

publicConnectionTypeService.description: A connection type service describes a certain type of action that a provider offers on this connection.

publicConnectionType.description: Contains all the information specified in a connection type.

tags.description: Describes additional properties or supported services of a connection type.

schemaRef.description: Reference to the schema describing this connection type.

schemaContent.description: Content of the schema describing this connection type.

linkedConnection.description: Describes to what other publicConnection a publicConnection is linked to. A publicConnection of type METADATA_IMPORT can be linked against one regular DI publicConnection (non METADATA_IMPORT) given they point to the same system. A regular DI publicConnection (non METADATA_IMPORT) can be the target of multiple connection links. Both connections must have matching connection type and root path/database name settings. If a publicConnection which is part of a connection link gets deleted, then the connection link(s) are automatically deleted. A link can either be set while creating a METADATA_IMPORT connection or by updating such connection. A link can be removed either by deleting one of the connections participating in a link or by updating the METADATA_IMPORT connection and omitting the connection link information in the update.

connectionId.description: Id of the connection a connection is linked to.

publicImportedDatasets.description: All datasets included in one import operation. Up to 2000 datasets can be imported per invocation.

publicExportedDatasets.description: All datasets included in one export operation.

publicRemoteObjectReference.description: Necessary information to access the referenced remote object

qualifiedName.description: Path to reach the remote runtime object through the SAP Data Intelligence connection. No encoding needed.

version.description: Timestamps for last modified and metadata last modified

publicDatasetConnection.description: Provides a description of the SAP Data Intelligence connection.

id.description: The SAP Data Intelligence connection ID

publicConnectionTypeEnum.description: Lists all supported connection types. This list is a subset of the types of the in the connection repository.

publicObjectGenericTypeEnum.description: The dataset generic type. For each generic type there is a canonical representation of the corresponding dataset.

publicDatasetTables.description: A table-based representation of the dataset. For table-based datasets, the representation consists of one single table.

publicDatasetTableDescription.description: Describes a dataset table representing the dataset.

attributes.description: List of attributes of the dataset table

uniqueKeys.description: List of unique keys of the dataset table. When a dataset table has a defined unique key, it means that (1) each row in the dataset table can be uniquely identified by the values of its unique key attributes, and (2) the set of unique key attributes determines all other attributes of the dataset table, and there are no subsets of the unique key attributes that have the same property. Unique key is a minimal set with respect to the functional dependency property.

publicAttribute.description: Describes a dataset attribute. An attribute represents a property or attribute in the initial dataset structure.

name.description: The name of the attribute. This name may override the name of the attribute in the original remote object.

originalName.description: The attribute name as defined in the remote object. The original name is given when it is different from the given name. This attribute can be helpful when the original name is an abbreviation and the given name is more verbose, for example.

qualifiedName.description: The name that identifies the represented property or attribute in the remote object. It is given when it is different from the given attribute name. Qualified names are needed for objects with complex structures, for example, graphs, SAP business objects, nested JSON or XML objects. The naming and format for this name is inferred by the canonical representation. No encoding needed.

length.description: When defined for a string, indicates the maximum size of the string.

precision.description: When defined for decimal, indicates the decimal precision.

scale.description: When defined for a decimal, indicates the decimal scale..

nativeDatatype.description: Data type as defined in the hosting system.

defaultAggregation.description: NONE indicates a normal field, for example, a dimension attribute for multidimensional dataset. != NONE indicates a measure and its default aggregation used when retrieving aggregated data.

labelAttribute.description: The name of the attribute label. This attribute corresponds to the label annotation in CDS model. A label is a human-readable text that is displayed besides input fields or as column headers. It can be used as a general information field to provide more context.

currencyAttribute.description: The name of the attribute providing the currency code to be attached to the amount given by this attribute. The currency code is taken from sap:unit or Measures.ISOCurrency.

unitAttribute.description: The name of the attribute providing the quantity unit code to be attached to the amount given by this attribute. The unit code is taken from sap:unit or Measures.unit.

format.description: For example, DD/MM/YYYY for dates or to specify the decimal delimiter of a number.

publicAttributeReferenceSet.description: The set of attributes of the same dataset. Those attributes are referenced by their qualified names.

publicDataTypeEnum.description: The attribute type in the DI type system.

publicDescriptions.description: The short or long texts describing the dataset.

publicRemoteObjectTypeEnum.description: All supported remote object types. Use IMPORT if the remote object type for an imported dataset is not listed.

publicVersion.description: Contains the version information for a dataset.

metadataVersionNumber.description: version number of the metadata (if no metadataLastModified is available). Version number is computed from available source information.

publicParameters.description: A list of all parameters belonging to a dataset.

publicParameter.description: A single parameter of a dataset.

name.description: Unique name that identifies the parameter

datatype.description: Data type as defined in the SAP Data Intelligence system.

originalDatatype.description: Data type as defined in the remote system.

length.description: When defined for a string, indicates the size of the string.

isMandatory.description: True indicates that the parameter value setting is mandatory.

possibleOperators.description: List of supported operators for setting values for this parameter. If empty or not provided, all operators are supported.

publicOperators.description: A single comparison operator such as =, <, or >

postDatasetsResponse.description: When posting the datasets to be imported, it returns the importTaskId that was created.

importTaskSummary.description: The summary report on the status of an importTask, including additional error information.

failedDatasetImport.description: Number of datasets that failed to import.

status.description: Overall status of the import task.

datasets.description: Individual status of all included datasets

errors.description: Detailed description of all import errors.

status.description: Overall status if the import task

userId.description: ID of the user who started the import task

qualifiedName.description: Qualified name of the dataset. No encoding needed.

isSuccessful.description: Indicates whether the import was successful

errorRef.description: Reference to an error, if any

publicErrorType.description: Different error types describing why a dataset import task may have failed.

importError.description: Error object for an import task

publicFactsheet.description: Collection of detailed information about a dataset

version.description: Version number of the public fact sheet.

compressed.description: Indicates if the fact sheet is compressed or not.

publicColumns.description: Details about the colum metadata of a table

rowCount.description: Number of rows in the dataset

uri.description: Uniform resource identifier of the dataset which is equivalent to the qualified name

size.description: Size of the dataset in bytes

colCount.description: Number of columns in the dataset

modifiedDate.description: Date when the dataset was modified the last time

charset.description: CSV specific setting for the character set of the file, such as UTF-8 or UTF-16

header.description: CSV specific setting that identifies how to treat the first column, column header, or data

colDelimiter.description: Specific CSV setting that shows a visual representation of the column delimitor such as , ; TAB

rowDelimiter.description: Specific CSV setting that shows a visual representation of the row delimitor such as CRLF, LF

textDelimiter.description: Specific CSV setting that shows a visual representation of the text delimitor such as ' ".

descriptions.description: Short or long texts describing the dataset

properties.description: Properties differentiating between the dataset and the remote dataset

connectionId.description: Connection ID where the dataset exists

connectionType.description: Connection type where the dataset exists

sampleRowCount.description: The number of rows that were sampled to get the profiling result. If equal to the row count, then the profile was done on the full dataset.

sampled.description: Indicates whether the profile was done on the full dataset or on a sample

type.description: Column data type as defined in the SAP Data Intelligence system.

scale.description: Number of digits to the right of the decimal point

avg.description: The average value of a numeric column. Only allowed for columns with a numeric data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

avgLen.description: The average length of the values in this column. Only allowed for columns with a string data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

blankPerc.description: The percentage of blank column values. Only allowed for columns with a string data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

countDist.description: The number of distinct column values, including null. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, boolean or date/time data type. If topDistinct is undefined then this property is undefined.

lowCardinality.description: If true, then this column contains a large number of repeated values. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, boolean, or date/time data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

max.description: Maximum value in this column. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, or date/time data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

maxLen.description: Length of the maximum value in this column. Only allowed for columns with a string data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

min.description: Minimum value in this column. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, or date/time data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

minLen.description: Length of the minimum value in this column. Only allowed for columns with a string data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

nullPerc.description: The percentage of null column values. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, boolean, or date/time data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

sparse.description: If true, the values in the column sparsely populated. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, boolean, or date/time data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

unique.description: If true, the populated values in the column are unique. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, boolean, or date/time data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

zeroPerc.description: The percentage of zero values in a numeric column. Only allowed for columns with a numeric data type. If there are no rows or the column only contains null, then this property is undefined.

descriptions.description: Short or long texts describing the dataset. For catalog objects this could include remote descriptions extracted from systems along with customer defined descriptions.

uniqueGroup.description: List of unique groups associated with the column. Group identifiers are numeric starting at 1

properties.description: Properties describing the column from the remote dataset

columns.description: Used to describe nested objects. For example, in the Excel file format the outer columns represent sheets and the inner columns represent columns in a sheet.

publicProfileColumnTopDistinct.description: Top distinct values for a column. Only allowed for columns with a string, numeric, boolean, or date/time data type. If there are no rows, then this property is undefined.

value.description: The value of the entry as found in the data set

count.description: The number of distinct column values, including null

publicAnnotation.description: Description or annotation for a dataset

value.description: The text of the description or annotation

origin.description: CATALOG for user defined descriptions and REMOTE for extracted from remote source.

publicComputationNode.description: A computation node represents the extracted data lineage from a computation specification. It describes the relationship between a set of input datasets, and a set of output datasets into which data items have been inserted, updated, or deleted from, when the executable object defined by the computation specification executes.

externalDatasets.description: Give information on runtime objects or design fragments objects which are referenced in the extracted lineage. This information can be partial

transforms.description: A computation node consists of a flow of transform operations that take dataset as input and produce dataset as output. Transform operations are introduced to facilitate the modular extraction of data lineage information from a computation.

outputDatasets.description: All datasets whose content are transformed by the transform operation.

datasetComputation.description: Describe the computation done by the transform operation.

publicDatasetComputation.description: Description of the computation of a dataset performed by a transform operation.

publicInternalDefinitionObject.description: Refers to the internal definition object (if it exists) that is represented by the lineage object within the extracted definition. By internal definition object we mean an internal object which is part of the whole definition. The type of definition object depends on the authoring model used for the extracted the definition. All this semantic information (objects types, ...) which is specific to the authoring model can be given in the additionInfo property through dedicated semantic properties. Examples of internal definition objects are business objects (part of a BI universe or BI report definition)

definitionObjectIdentifier.description: A string identifying the object in the definition.

publicIdentityComputation.description: An identity computation is a computation that links one 'input dataset' to one 'output dataset'. A computation is an identity computation - if - as the result of the computation, the content of the output dataset is the same as the content of the input dataset.

publicBlackBoxDatasetComputation.description: Describes a blackbox computation that computes one (or several) output datasets. We distinguish two types of blackbox computation : In a 'FULL_BLACKBOX' some outputs and some inputs datasets are unknown. In a 'INPUT_BLACKBOX' computation, the outputs are known but both the attribute lineage and some input datasets are unknown. In a 'COMPUTATION_BLACKBOX' outputs and input datasets are known but no attribute lineage is provided. When no output datasets are provided, the blackbox computation describes a coarse grain lineage information (no attribute level lineage)

publicComputationCommand.description: Describes the computation command executed inside the blackbox.

language.description: The general type of language used to write the command. We currently support SQL.HANA, SQL.VORA, SQL.MYSQL, SQL.MSSQL, SQL.DB2, SQL.ORACLE, SQL.BIGQUERY, SQL.SAP_IQ, SQL.HDL_DB, SQL.REDSHIFT. For generic SQL, it will just be SQL.

connectionId.description: Connection id through which the command is executed.

defaultSQLSchema.description: Default schema for unqualified names referenced in formula using a SQL language.

publicTransformOutputReference.description: A dataset whose content is transformed by a transform operation.

publicComputationReference.description: A dataset which is referenced by a computation.

alias.description: Alias used in the formula to refers to the input dataset.

publicExternalDatasetReference.description: Refers to the full-qualified name of the dataset which is a combination of its connection id and its qualified name.

publicIdentityComputationType.description: An ID operation maintains the same schema between the input and output dataset.

publicBlackBoxDatasetComputationType.description: In a 'INPUT_BLACKBOX' computation, the outputs are known but both the attribute lineage and some input datasets are unknown. In a 'FULL_BLACKBOX' some outputs and some inputs datasets are unknown. In a 'COMPUTATION_BLACKBOX', outputs and input datasets are known but no attribute lineage is provided.

publicUpdateMode.description: Describe the way the output dataset content is changed after the execution of the content. We distinguish the following update Mode: (1) DEFINE: data items produced by the transform operation exclusively define the contents of the output dataset. (2) REPLACE: all data items of the output dataset are replaced by the data items produced by the transform operation.(3) UPDATE: data items produced by the transform operation are used to update some data items of the output dataset.(4) UPSERT: data items produced by the transform operation are used to either update existing data items or insert new data items in the output dataset.(5) INSERT: data items produced by the transform operation are used to insert new data items in the output dataset.(6) DELETE: data items produced by the transform operation are removed from the output dataset.

publicDatasetIds.description: One or multiple external identifier(s) that identify a referenced dataset. Multiple external identifiers can point to the same dataset as it potentially can be reached through several different connections. One or several external identifier(s) will be used to refer to the dataset when describing lineage at computation node level.

name.description: Unique name that identifies the dataset in the system.

exposesMetadata.description: Indicate whether the referenced dataset exposes its metadata.

publicLineageOwner.description: Describe the object defining the computation. It can be a reference to an existing extracted system artifact or either a computation system artifact. It is defined via its full-qualified name which is a combination of the connection id and the qualified name of the to-be-created artifact.

fullQualifiedName.description: Full-qualified name of the lineage owner which is a combination of the connection id and the qualified name.

publicComputationSystemArtifact.description: Given if the lineage owner is not an extractable artifact. It describes the system artifact which is not a dataset that defines the computation node.

name.description: Name of the artifact defining the computation.

nativeType.description: Type of native object (vFlow, procedure, etc).

owner.description: Owner of the object in the extracted system

publicVersionInfo.description: The versioning information beloning to a computation system artifact.

nativeVersion.description: Indicates the version of the object in the hosting system. If the hosting system does not handle versions, this field is not defined.

versionNumber.description: Integer value that represents the version of the object from which lineage has been extracted. This version number will be used by the metadata service to compare versions. Each extractor may have his own strategy for generating version numbers depending on how the version information is managed by the underlying system. For example, version could be a version number extracted from the system or timestamps converted in integer value. If the version number is obtained from a native version, the version number must follows the ordering implied by native version format.

publicConnectionReference.description: Refers to the object in a connection. This connection can be a BDH connection or a remote connection.

qualifiedName.description: Path to reach the remote runtime object through the connection.

connectionId.description: Identifier of the BDH connection to access the dataset.

remoteConnectionRefId.description: Refers to the remote connection that is used to access the dataset.

PublicPassPercent.description: Percentage of records that passed all rules in a category

PublicFailPercent.description: Percentage of records that failed any rules in a category

PublicPassDelta.description: Change in pass percent from previous run

PublicRecordsFiltered.description: Number of records that were filtered out and not processed by rule conditions

PublicFilterPercent.description: Percentage of records that were filtered out and not processed by rule in a category

PublicFilterDelta.description: Change in filter percent from previous run

PublicDatasetParameterSetting.description: The dataset parameter setting used when saving a rule dataset contains parameters

PublicDatasetInput.description: Dataset information to re-use in a rulebook when adding multiple bindings

description.description: Description of the dataset that is pulled from the connection

rulebookId.description: Rulebook ID to map the dataset to

PublicDatasetResult.description: A rules dataset result with the ID of the dataset

isPrepareable.description: Indicates if data prepration is possible for this dataset

PublicRulesByCategory.description: List of categories with rules in that category

CategoryWithPublicRules.description: Category information with rules in that category

GetPublicRules.description: Array of rules returned in a GET call

createdByUI.description: Displayable username of the user that created the rule

lastModifiedBy.description: Last user to modify the rule

lastModifiedByUI.description: Displayable username of the last user to modify the rule

terms.description: Terms that are related to the rule

CreatePublicRule.description: Information needed to create a new rule

GetPublicRule.description: A rule object returned on a GET call

rulebookIds.description: A list of rulebook Ids and names that reference the rule

GetPublicRuleByIdResult.description: A rule object returned on a GET on /rules/{ruleId}

ruleExpression.description: Rule conditions specified in advanced script syntax

filterExpression.description: Rule filters specified in advanced script syntax.

line.description: Line number where error was identified

column.description: Column number where error was identified

previousName.description: Previous name of the parameter in case of name change

type.description: Data Intelligence data type of the parameter

contentType.description: Content type of the parameter (currently unused)

caseInsensitive.description: Condition compares case sensitive or insensitive for content in this parameter for type STRING

trim.description: Trim pre and post white space before comparing content in this parameter for type STRING

isValuesListParameter.description: Flag to control if valuesList contains references to input parameters or hard coded values to compare against

conditionName.description: Name used in grouping of conditions together

createdByUI.description: Displayable username of the user that created the rulebook

lastModifiedBy.description: Last user to modify the rulebook

lastModifiedByUI.description: Displayable username of the last user to modify the rulebook

numRules.description: Number of rules in the rulebook

numRuns.description: Number of times the rulebook has been executed

status.description: Status of category that takes into account all statuses of rules in category

refId.description: ID of the rulebook reference to rule

status.description: Status of rule that takes into account the status of the bindings

PublicRuleBookBindingsDetails.description: List of bindings for a rule

description.description: Description of the dataset that is pulled from the connection

config.description: Mappings between rulebook rule parameters and dataset column names

createdByUI.description: Displayable username of the user that created the rulebook

lastModifiedBy.description: Last user to modify the rulebook

lastModifiedByUI.description: Displayable username of the last user to modify the rulebook

numRules.description: Number of rules in the rulebook

status.description: The latest completed rulebook run status. undefined if no rulebook runs

passPercent.description: Pass percent for the rulebook run. undefined if no rulebook runs

previousFailedRecordSettings.description: The failed record configuration used during the last completed run of the rulebook

recordsPass.description: Number of records that passed all rules in category

recordsFail.description: Number of records that failed any rules in category

recordsPass.description: Number of records that passed a rule

recordsFail.description: Number of records that failed a rule

sample.description: If there is any sample data to display for the rule binding

column.description: Column name that parameter is bound to

qualifiedName.description: Qualified name of object on connection

recordsPass.description: Number of records that passed all rules in a dataset

recordCountDelta.description: Difference between records from previous run

recordsPass.description: Number of records that passed all rules

recordCountDelta.description: Difference between records from previous run

savedFailedRecords.description: Flag to identify if all failed records were saved to external target

recordsPass.description: Number of records that passed all rules

index.description: Corresponding index in results that stores full data

savedFailedRecords.description: Flag to identify if all failed records were saved to external target

createdByUI.description: Displayable username of the user that created the rulebook

lastModifiedBy.description: Last user to modify the rulebook

lastModifiedByUI.description: Displayable username of the last user to modify the rulebook

enabled.description: Indicates if saving failed records was enabled

mode.description: Mode that was used during the last completed rulebook run

connectionId.description: Failed record connection id that was used during the last completed rulebook run

container.description: Parent path of failed record folder that was used during the last completed rulebook run

description.description: rich text description of term. if relationshipType is BASIC then some text may be truncated if too long. In addition, empty, img and table tags are removed.

customInputs.description: custom values defined by glossary template, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

relatedObjects.description: objects related to the term, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

keywords.description: glossary terms keywords, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

synonyms.description: glossary terms synonyms, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

categories.description: categories bound to the term, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

createdBy.description: user that created the object, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

createDate.description: date object was created, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

lastModifiedDate.description: date object was last modified, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

lastModifiedBy.description: last user to modify the object, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

createdByUI.description: displayable username of the user that created the object, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

lastModifiedByUI.description: displayable username of the last user to modify the object, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

status.description: term status, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

errors.description: list of issues that need to be resolved, only used if relationshipType is DEFINE

PublicTermRelationshipType.description: type of relationship between the term and the related object

values.description: values of custom input can be a single value or multiple ones if that input supports it

PublicTermRelatedObjects.description: related objects to term organized in a graph with nodes, groups and edges that connect nodes

nodes.description: nodes that represent glossary terms, dataset, or columns in datasets

groups.description: groups node belongs to, undefined if it is not in a group

info.description: extra display info for the object. populated for datasets and columns

indirect.description: Flag to identify if a related term has a indirect relationship. Indirect terms are not saved or modified.

obfuscate.description: optional flag to signal that user does not have permission and all sensitive info is removed from object

remoteObjectType.description: type of remote object, for example VIEW, TABLE, FILE.CSV

type.description: type of column, for example string

originalDataType.description: original type in source connection for example nvarchar

descriptions.description: short or long texts describing the column/dataset

type.description: less than 60 characters is a SHORT description. anything longer is LONG

parentId.description: parent id of dataset in catalog

PublicRulebookRelatedObjectInfo.description: Info for a related rule book

status.description: status of the rule book. 'ERROR' if any rules are in the error state.

from.description: node id that edge originates from

to.description: node id that edge ends at

obfuscate.description: optional flag to signal that user does not have permission and all sensitive info is removed from object

PublicTermOther.description: glossary term's keyword or synonym output

id.description: id of the keyword or synonym

PublicGlossaryOther.description: keyword or synonym found in the glossary

PublicErrorGlossaryCustomColumnParams.description: Set of optional parameters that can be used by the UI to generate a localized version of the error message for custom input columns issues

type.description: object type the input will validate and store

min.description: minimum value if range checking is done on input

max.description: maximum value if range checking is done on input

format.description: date format to which the value doesn't conform

PublicScheduleTaskOutBase.description: Details about a single scheduled task

lastModifiedBy.description: Last user to modify the object

executeAs.description: User who is impersonated when running the scheduled task

runCount.description: Number of times the schedule has been run

runs.description: Detailed information about the last 0-5 runs

task.description: Task information determined by the taskType

PublicScheduleTaskOut.description: Details about a single scheduled task

runTime.description: Milliseconds the scheduled task has run

task.description: task information for the run determined by the taskType

extractionId.description: The extraction identifier, if available in the monitoring view

profileCfgId.description: id of the table row that is storing the scheduled dataset profiling configuration

qualifiedName.description: the qualified name of the remote dataset

PublicScheduleTaskRulebookIn.description: Details about the scheduled rulebook processing

failedRecordMode.description: Mode when saving records, append or overwrite failed record data

executionId.description: Rulebook run identifier, if available in the monitoring view or rulebook results

PublicScheduledRunTaskProfileOut.description: details about the scheduled profiling run

PublicScheduleTaskTypeOut.description: Details about the scheduled task type

PublicScheduledRunTaskTypeOut.description: Details about the scheduled task type

PublicScheduleConfig.description: Details required to create a schedule

dateStart.description: Schedule start date, if omitted then it becomes effective immediately

dateEnd.description: Schedule end date, if omitted then it continues indefinitely

enabled.description: Determines if the schedule is active

container.description: Parent path of failed record folder

filteredByPolicy.description: Flag to identify whether the failed record location is configured, but the user does not have the policy permissions to view the connection

tagList.description: The list of tag IDs to use for tagging

tagId.description: The object containing a tag ID

publicTaggingModeEnum.description: possible tagging modes: USER: This tag has been set by a user EXTRACTED: This tag has been obtained from an extraction RULES: This tag has been created by a rule AUTO-TAGGING: This tag has been obtained using an auto tagging algorithm PUBLICATION: This tag has been propagated from a publication tag.

canChangeTagging.description: Indicates whether the tagging can be updated

tags.description: The list of tags of this hierarchy used to tag the object.

attributeQualifiedName.description: The qualified name that identifies the attribute in the dataset

name.description: The new value for the tag name. Should not contain the '.' character because it is the tag path separator.

description.description: The new value for the description

tagCreationInput.description: The input for tag creation inside a hierarchy

parentId.description: Identifies the parent tag in the hierarchy

name.description: The name of the tag. Should not contain the '.' character because it is the tag path separator.

description.description: The string description of the tag

publicDeleteResponse.description: The number of datasets and attributes for which tags have been removed due to the deletion

deletedTags.description: The number of deleted tags. The actual number could be more if a whole subtree is deleted.

datasetsWithDeletedTags.description: The number of datasets whose tags have been removed because the corresponding tag was deleted. This can be more than 0 when the force flag = true.

attributesWithDeletedTags.description: The number of attributes whose tags have been removed because the corresponding tag was deleted. This can be more than 0 when the force flag = true.

postTagResponse.description: The response requests that post a tag either on a dataset or an attribute

replacedAncestorTag.description: The tag using an ancestor tag that has been replaced (if any)

redundantWithDescendantTags.description: If the request tag is redundant with one or more tags using a descendant tag (given here), the requested tag is not added

tagHierarchyHeader.description: The name and description of a hierarchy

name.description: The name of the tag hierarchy. The name should not contain special characters.

description.description: The description of the tag hierarchy

publicAllowedActivities.description: The list of allowed activity on a tag hierarchy.

canUseForTagging.description: Indicates whether the request user can use the hierarchy for tagging. Imported hierarchies cannot be used for tagging. All other hierarchies can be used for tagging.

canModify.description: Indicates whether the hierarchy can be modified by the user. Imported hierarchies cannot be modified. All other hierarchies can be modified.

canDoTaggingApproval.description: Indicates whether the request user can approve tagging made from tags of this hierarchy. Tagging approval cannot be done on imported hierarchies. Tagging approval can be done on all other hierarchies.

id.description: The ID of the tag hierarchy

createdBy.description: The user who created the hierarchy. Undefined if the hierarchy is imported.

lastModifiedBy.description: The user who modified the hierarchy. Undefined if the hierarchy is imported.

taggedDatasets.description: The number of datasets tagged with tags from this hierarchy. If an attribute of a dataset is tagged, then the dataset is counted as a tagged dataset.

taggedAttributes.description: The number of attributes tagged with tags from this hierarchy

lastModificationDate.description: The date the hierarchy was last changed

matchingName.description: The tag or hierarchy name that matches the search wildcard expression.

tagId.description: Shows the tag ID description when the matching name corresponds to a tag.

tagPath.description: Shows the path of the tag when the matching name corresponds to a tag.

tagHierarchyWithMatchInfo.description: The result of a search on the available tag hierarchies

matchingInfo.description: Shows the matched name or tags in hierarchies when they are filtered with a search pattern screen.

parentId.description: Identifies the parent tag in the hierarchy

normalizedName.description: The normalized name. Lower case and consecutive blanks are removed.

path.description: The tag path in the hierarchy

description.description: The string description of the tag

lastModifiedBy.description: The ID of the last user who changed the name or description of the tag.

lastModificationDate.description: The date that the name or description of the tag was modified.

taggedDatasets.description: The number of datasets tagged with this tag or its descendants

taggedAttributes.description: The number of attributes directly used by this tag or by one of its descendants.

tagHierarchyWithContent.description: The complete description of a hierarchy

AnnotationOrigin.description: we distinguish annotations extracted from remote sources (REMOTE) from those given in the catalog (CATALOG)

AnnotationType.description: we distinguish SHORT annotations ( less than 60 characters) from others (LONG)

errorCauses.description: list of causes for an exception

code.description: Usually a 5 digit code, but can be a string as well from 3rd party libraries. When used with the module name, this provides a unique value that can be used to look up information on the error.

guid.description: unique identifier to find a specific entry in a log file instead of all entries with the same code

hostname.description: host where the log file is located

code.description: Usually a 5 digit code, but can be a string as well from 3rd party libraries. When used with the module name, this provides a unique value that can be used to look up information on the error.

guid.description: unique identifier to find a specific entry in a log file instead of all entries with the same code

hostname.description: host where the log file is located

causes.description: list of causes for this error

taskHandles.description: list of vflow handles associated with the error that can be used to lookup additional details

property.description: An instance of a property. An property consists of the property identifier and the property value. A property identifier consists of a namespace and name.  Note that some properties could be multi-valued. In this case, several instances of the same property will be given

namespace.description: namespace to which belong the property. Name spaces are hierarchical

name.description: name of the property. Is unique inside a name space

errorThreshold.description: percentage that is between error and warning

passThreshold.description: percentage that is between warning and pass

topExportDatasets.name: $top
topExportDatasets.description: Max number of datasets to return

topExportPublicComputationNodes.name: $top
topExportPublicComputationNodes.description: Max number of public computation nodes to return

top.name: $top
top.description: Max number of entries to return

skip.name: $skip
skip.description: indicates the number of items to skip

count.name: $count
count.description: If true, returns the total number of entries available as count in the response (not the page size)
